exams.title: Oxford Placement Test - Practice Set (CEFR Evaluation)
exams.description: A shorter diagnostic exam consisting of grammar, listening, and reading questions. Best for quick evaluations.

questions.text: I'm a student ____ university. I'm studying English.
There is a green scarf on the chair, ____ is it?
I don't know if they missed me or my cooking. I had ___ walked through the door when they were demanding supper!
The tutor says it's always better to ____ personal experience for inspiration.
My brother works ___ a large software company.
We arrived ___ the airport two hours before our flight.
She isn't interested ___ watching horror movies.
This is the restaurant ___ we first met.
If I ___ enough money, I'd buy a new laptop.
You should look ___ the meaning of that word.
Listen to the dialogue. What does the man imply?
questions.audioText: Man: I can barely keep my eyes open after that meeting. Woman: Go get some coffee, we still have another session in ten minutes.

options.c: He is planning to go to sleep now.

questions.text: Listen to the dialogue. What does the woman regret?
questions.audioText: Woman: I wish I'd listened to your advice. Man: Well, it's too late to worry about that now, we just have to fix the damage.

options.a: She plans to follow the advice later.
options.b: She regrets not listening to the man's advice.
options.c: She forgot what the advice was.

questions.text: Listen to the announcement. What is the cause of the train delay?
questions.audioText: Attention passengers, the ten fifteen train to London Victoria has been delayed by approximately twenty minutes due to signaling problems. We apologize for the delay.

questions.passage: A grey morning in Hong Kong and I'm just setting off to photograph Courtney Gordon. In recent years she's come to be known as the 'strongest woman on Earth'. As a 13-year-old secondary school student growing up in Edinburgh, Scotland, she won the gold medal in the national archery competition. Not content to settle for success in one sport, however, she later became the national women's shot-put champion. It's safe to say Gordon is big news in the world of sport, having twice won the annual Women's World Weightlifting Competition.
questions.text: According to the text, Courtney Gordon first achieved national success in which sport?

questions.passage: A grey morning in Hong Kong and I'm just setting off to photograph Courtney Gordon. In recent years she's come to be known as the 'strongest woman on Earth'. As a 13-year-old secondary school student growing up in Edinburgh, Scotland, she won the gold medal in the national archery competition. Not content to settle for success in one sport, however, she later became the national women's shot-put champion. It's safe to say Gordon is big news in the world of sport, having twice won the annual Women's World Weightlifting Competition.
questions.text: What is the writer's main purpose in this passage?

options.a: To describe Courtney Gordon's physical appearance.
options.b: To trace the development of Courtney Gordon's sporting career.
options.c: To explain why weightlifting is a rapidly growing sport.

exams.title: Oxford Placement Test - Set B (Full Evaluation)
exams.description: The official comprehensive Oxford Placement Test containing a full bank of Grammar, dialogue-based situations, Listening tasks, and Courtney Gordon Reading modules.

questions.text: I'm a student ____ university. I'm studying English.
There is a green scarf on the chair, ____ is it?
I don't know if they missed me or my cooking. I had ___ walked through the door when they were demanding supper!
The tutor says it's always better to ____ personal experience for inspiration.
Very much so, ____ it provides further evidence on the link between greener lifestyles and well-being.
I did a lot of preparation and ___ for the bad weather, I would've made it to the top.
It's time ___ a new car.
Go for it. Don't let that hold you ____
My brother works ___ a large software company.
We arrived ___ the airport two hours before our flight.
She isn't interested ___ watching horror movies.
This is the restaurant ___ we first met.
If I ___ enough money, I'd buy a new laptop.
You should look ___ the meaning of that word.
They have lived here ___ 2018.
The train was delayed, ___ we arrived late.
I haven't seen him ___ a long time.
Please turn ___ the lights before leaving.
We were looking forward ___ everyone at the reunion.
Hardly had I sat down ___ the phone rang.
Listen to the dialogue. What does the wife mean by her first comment?
questions.audioText: Wife: Had we known that they were going to build a motorway so close to us, I'd have thought twice about us buying this house.

options.a: I wish we hadn't bought this house.
options.b: We ought to think again about buying this house.
options.c: You should have reconsidered buying this house.

questions.text: Listen to the dialogue. What does the man mean?
questions.audioText: Man: If I'd remembered her birthday, she wouldn't have been so upset.

options.a: He forgot her birthday and she was upset.
options.b: He remembered her birthday too late.
options.c: She wasn't upset because he remembered.

questions.text: Listen to the dialogue. What does the woman mean?
questions.audioText: Woman: It's about time we replaced this old washing machine.

options.a: The washing machine is still quite new.
options.b: The washing machine should be replaced now.
options.c: The washing machine was replaced recently.

questions.text: Listen to the dialogue. What does the man mean?
questions.audioText: Man: I can barely keep my eyes open after that meeting.

questions.text: Listen to the dialogue. What does the woman mean?
questions.audioText: Woman: I wish I'd listened to your advice.

options.a: She plans to follow the advice later.

questions.passage: A grey morning in Hong Kong and I'm just setting off to photograph Courtney Gordon. In recent years she's come to be known as the 'strongest woman on Earth'. As a 13-year-old secondary school student growing up in Edinburgh, Scotland, she won the gold medal in the national archery competition. Not content to settle for success in one sport, however, she later became the national women's shot-put champion. It's safe to say Gordon is big news in the world of sport, having twice won the annual Women's World Weightlifting Competition. Her success has made her the face of this rapidly growing sport.
questions.text: What does the phrase 'strongest woman on Earth' refer to Courtney Gordon's success in?

questions.passage: A grey morning in Hong Kong and I'm just setting off to photograph Courtney Gordon. In recent years she's come to be known as the 'strongest woman on Earth'. As a 13-year-old secondary school student growing up in Edinburgh, Scotland, she won the gold medal in the national archery competition. Not content to settle for success in one sport, however, she later became the national women's shot-put champion. It's safe to say Gordon is big news in the world of sport, having twice won the annual Women's World Weightlifting Competition. Her success has made her the face of this rapidly growing sport.
questions.text: How old was Courtney Gordon when she won the gold medal in the national archery competition?
